results.intent: Authentic French fine dining, classical technique, not fusion or modernist, proper wine pairings

restaurant.name: Alo
restaurant.address: 163 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5V 2L6, Canada

relevantReviews.author: Matteo Farrokhi
relevantReviews.excerpt: The tasting menu is outstanding — every dish is beautifully presented and full of flavor, with a perfect balance of French technique and creativity
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: This review highlights the classical French technique in the food, aligning with the user's intent

relevantReviews.excerpt: The wine pairings, though, were a letdown, most of the reds, whites, and even the champagne were disappointing
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: This review specifically addresses the wine pairings, a crucial aspect of the user's intent

relevantReviews.author: Anthony DiCenzo
relevantReviews.excerpt: Service is world-class: gracious, polished, attentive without hovering
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: This review praises the service, which is important for a fine dining experience focused on classical technique and proper wine pairings

analysis.explanation: For someone looking for authentic French fine dining with classical technique and proper wine pairings, this restaurant scores slightly lower than Google's 4.6 because 4 of 5 reviewers praised the food quality and service, with sentiments indicating excellent execution of French technique and world-class service. However, the dimension of drink quality, specifically wine pairings, received a negative sentiment from 1 reviewer, which directly impacts the user's intent. Google's high rating is driven by scores on ambiance and portion size, which the user deprioritized, highlighting the importance of personalized scoring for specific dining preferences.

decomposed.author: Korey Francis

dimensions.claim: I did not know eggs could taste so deep and rich. I have never had steak so succulent and buttery.
I went there famished and I left stuffed
The service was impeccable. Staff are attentive, knowledgeable and conversational without being overbearing.
The experience sitting counterside and watching the chefs prepare the dishes was like getting dinner and a show

decomposed.text:
The food is so good it gave me two existential crises mid meal. I did not know eggs could taste so deep and rich. I have never had steak so succulent and buttery. The bread is perfect. Even the smaller bites were bursting with unique and complex flavor combinations that I had never imagined.

We got the tasting menu. I went there famished and I left stuffed. In total I believe we were in the restaurant for about 3 hours.

The experience sitting counterside and watching the chefs prepare the dishes was like getting dinner and a show. If you're like me and you have an interest in kitchen craft from a childhood watching Food Network then I recommend this option.

The service was impeccable. Staff are attentive, knowledgeable and conversational without being overbearing.

Weeks later, I am still thinking about this wonderful meal.

decomposed.author: Matteo Farrokhi

dimensions.claim: The tasting menu is outstanding — every dish is beautifully presented and full of flavor, with a perfect balance of French technique and creativity
Service is professional, friendly, and very knowledgeable, making the whole experience even more enjoyable
From the moment you walk in, everything feels refined and perfectly organized

decomposed.text:
One of the best dining experiences in Toronto. From the moment you walk in, everything feels refined and perfectly organized.

The tasting menu is outstanding — every dish is beautifully presented and full of flavor, with a perfect balance of French technique and creativity. The attention to detail in each course truly reflects Michelin-level quality.

Service is professional, friendly, and very knowledgeable, making the whole experience even more enjoyable.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for a luxurious and memorable fine dining experience

decomposed.author: Mark Erdmann

dimensions.claim: The best meal I have had easily
We had the 10 course tasting menu and it was the perfect amount of food

decomposed.text: The best meal I have had easily. Everything was just about perfect. We had the 10 course tasting menu and it was the perfect amount of food. Mostly seafood focused with just a couple heavier and richer dishes to balance it out. Amazing!

decomposed.author: Zoe

dimensions.claim: The food was good, but nothing really surprised us
The wine pairings, though, were a letdown, most of the reds, whites, and even the champagne were disappointing
The service was very thoughtful and wonderful overall

decomposed.text:
Came here to celebrate a birthday, we had the 10 course set menu with wine pairings.

The food was good, but nothing really surprised us. Each dish was well balanced, just not very memorable. The wine pairings, though, were a letdown, most of the reds, whites, and even the champagne were disappointing. We also felt the sommelier didn’t quite match the level you’d expect from a Michelin star restaurant.

although we had some disappointment, the service was very thoughtful and wonderful overall.

decomposed.author: Anthony DiCenzo

dimensions.claim: The execution is flawless, the flavors are balanced yet surprising, and every plate feels like it was composed by people who care about perfection without ever drifting into pretension
Service is world-class: gracious, polished, attentive without hovering
The room itself feels quietly luxurious — intimate, atmospheric, and perfectly suited to the multi-course format

decomposed.text:
An exceptional dining experience — easily deserving its Michelin star (and arguably more).

Alo is one of those rare restaurants where absolutely everything — from the first greeting to the final bite — feels intentional, precise, and quietly extraordinary. Toronto has no shortage of ambitious tasting-menu restaurants, but Alo sits in a class of its own. The execution is flawless, the flavors are balanced yet surprising, and every plate feels like it was composed by people who care about perfection without ever drifting into pretension.

The tasting menu flows beautifully: thoughtful transitions, impeccable pacing, and dishes that manage to be both technically impressive and genuinely delicious. There’s no “one dish” that steals the show because the entire experience is the show — elevated but warm, sophisticated but deeply satisfying. Even the smallest elements (a broth, a garnish, a sauce) are dialed in with a level of finesse you rarely see.

Service is world-class: gracious, polished, attentive without hovering. The kind of hospitality that anticipates needs rather than reacts to them. The team operates with the smoothness of a well-rehearsed ensemble, and yet the energy is relaxed, never stiff.

The room itself feels quietly luxurious — intimate, atmospheric, and perfectly suited to the multi-course format. It sets the stage rather than competing with the food.

Alo absolutely earns its Michelin star, and honestly, it’s easy to imagine it holding more. It’s a restaurant that makes you feel lucky to be there.

5 out of 5 stars — a standout in Toronto and a must for anyone who loves exceptional dining.

restaurant.address: 12 Upper St Martin's Ln, London WC2H 9FB, UK

relevantReviews.author: Thomas Bloom
relevantReviews.excerpt: the whole place is very busy but buzzing with good vibes and atmosphere
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: this review highlights the ambiance of the restaurant, which is a key aspect of the user's intent

relevantReviews.author: A Luk
relevantReviews.excerpt: lineup was considerable but they bring out chai tea to the waiting customers
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: this review mentions the wait time and the quality of the drinks, both of which are relevant to the user's intent

relevantReviews.author: KJ L
relevantReviews.excerpt: tasty and flavourful indian cuisine
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: this review praises the food quality, which is a key aspect of the user's intent

However, Google's high rating is driven by service scores, which you deprioritized. The wait time, although a concern for some reviewers, is mitigated by the complimentary chai tea served to waiting customers, making the overall experience more enjoyable.

decomposed.author: Thomas Bloom

dimensions.claim: the wait for the restaurant is 25-30 minutes
the whole place is very busy but buzzing with good vibes and atmosphere
an army of well organized servers are constantly around cleaning serving and taking orders
the food itself was one of the best experiences of Indian food I've ever had
toilets were very nicely decorated and very clean
the menu is a moderate size , a good selection of everything

decomposed.text:
Ok the important things , you can book after 6 pm provided the group is 6 people or more.
Around 7 to 8 pm on a Friday night at least , the wait for the restaurant is 25-30 minutes.
Free tea is served at random for the queueing customers.

The restaurant is on 2 floors , each floor is huge . The lower floor seems the more quiet part. The whole place is very busy but buzzing with good vibes and atmosphere. An army of well organized servers are constantly around cleaning serving and taking orders. Indeed our server was a well spoken knowledgeable gentleman.

The menu is a moderate size , a good selection of everything.
The food itself was one of the best experiences of Indian food I've ever had , and I've been to a lot of places in the UK and india .
The spice levels are fortunately catered for the Indian palate . So westerners beware lol
Toilets were very nicely decorated and very clean , a bit oldly worldy in design

You must at least try this restaurant once in your life! I'll definitely come back here.

decomposed.author: KJ L

dimensions.claim: tasty and flavourful indian cuisine
great service as well
a great selection of vegetarian options available
complimentary molten chocolate cake

decomposed.text:
Recommended to try at least once on your trip! Tasty and flavourful indian cuisine - different from the usual. We were on a vegetarian diet that evening and there's a great selection of vegetarian options available. Although it may lean towards the saltier side. For people who prefer less sodium in your diets, yoy may wish to try requesting for less salt and see if it could be accommodated.
Great service as well! The server presented us with a complimentary molten chocolate cake as it was my birthday. Thankful for the kind gesture!

decomposed.author: A Luk

dimensions.claim: overall i think the food is good and tasty
the portions are small
i’m not entirely convinced the price tag is worth it
lineup was considerable

decomposed.text: Overall I think the food is good and tasty, though I’m not entirely convinced the price tag is worth it as the portions are small. Lineup was considerable but they bring out chai tea to the waiting customers. As I don’t recall the specific dish names, it’ll be a bit generic. Shrimp: dry but flavourful. Okra fries: well seasoned, crispy. Baked rice: average. Chicken curry: rich, recommended. Salad: not very impressive.

decomposed.author: Saleh Alajlan

dimensions.claim: the service was exceptional
no waiting at all
the food was generally average
a relaxed dining experience

decomposed.text:
I visited Dishoom around 16:00 and was pleasantly surprised by how smooth the experience was, no booking required and no waiting at all, which was a big plus.

The service was exceptional. Staff were attentive, friendly, and efficient throughout the meal, which really elevated the overall experience.

As for the food, it was generally average. Some dishes were enjoyable, but nothing particularly stood out as memorable. Unfortunately, the salad was disappointing and didn’t match the quality or flavour of the other items on the table.

Overall, Dishoom delivered excellent service and a relaxed dining experience, but the food itself felt hit-or-miss. Worth visiting for the service and convenience, though the menu could be more consistent.

decomposed.author: Don Eatsalot

dimensions.claim: i had higher hopes for liking this place
didn't feel worth the high price point
service was very good
they have two kids meals

decomposed.text: I had higher hopes for liking this place but may have ordered poorly - going for their most popular dishes rather than things I knew I liked. They have two kids meals and we tried both. The chicken was excellent and the paneer was disappointing. The fried okra, gunpowder potatoes and pau bhaji were good. The chicken Ruby was decent and if you like butter chicken will probably be a hit. The black daal, I didn't like but I don't usually like black daal so I can't fault the restaurant here. The naan and roti were good but didn't feel worth the high price point. We had a mezcal margarita that tasted more sweet than smokey and a glass of wine that was fine. Overall, I was underwhelmed but nothing was bad. I'm tempted to go back and try some different items. A service fee is automatically added (service was very good). Food came out quickly. We didn't have a reservation but showed up a little before 5 on a Saturday and were seated within 20 minutes.

results.intent: Authentic Ethiopian food, traditional injera, real spice blends, communal eating, not westernized

restaurant.name: The Spicy Ethiopian
restaurant.address: 175 Queen St E, Toronto, ON M5A 1S2, Canada

relevantReviews.author: Simarjot Sidhu
relevantReviews.excerpt: The menu has an amazing selection, and everything we tried was outstanding. The injera and lentils were absolutely amazing, pairs perfectly together.
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: This review is relevant because it mentions the authenticity and quality of the Ethiopian food, specifically the injera and lentils.

relevantReviews.author: M. N.
relevantReviews.excerpt: The food at this restaurant is top of the list for among the best I've ever eaten. It is quite spicy, as the name implies, but not overbearing.
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: This review is relevant because it mentions the quality and authenticity of the Ethiopian food, specifically the spice level.

relevantReviews.author: Shelby Young
relevantReviews.excerpt: The food was insane!!! This is my first experience with Ethiopian food, we drove from Niagara... and were not disappointed.
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: This review is relevant because it mentions the quality and authenticity of the Ethiopian food, specifically the reviewer's first experience with it.

analysis.explanation: For someone looking for authentic Ethiopian food with traditional injera and real spice blends, this restaurant scores higher than Google's 4.9 because 3 of 5 reviewers praised the food quality and authenticity, with specific mentions of the injera and spice level. However, Google's high rating is also driven by service and ambiance scores, which this user deprioritized. The Parallax score is 0.2 higher than Google's due to the emphasis on food quality and authenticity.

decomposed.author: Shelby Young

dimensions.claim: This restaurant is beautiful!!! So much care into the design
Our server was so kind and warm. The owner made us feel so welcomed and loved
The food was insane!!! ... the taste was incredible
And the coffee has ruined me for all other coffee. It was the best I have ever had!

decomposed.text:
This restaurant is beautiful!!! So much care into the design and food. You can feel the love and intent as soon as you walk in. We were greeted as soon as we walked in, had everything explained to us and given recommendations. Our server was so kind and warm. The owner made us feel so welcomed and loved.
The food was insane!!! This is my first experience with Ethiopian food, we drove from Niagara... and were not disappointed.  Beautifully played and lovingly made... the taste was incredible.
And the coffee has ruined me for all other coffee. It was the best I have ever had! Planning another trip ASAP

decomposed.author: M. N.

dimensions.claim: The restaurant has a beautiful ambience, very nice, warm decor
the food at this restaurant is top of the list for among the best I've ever eaten
Excellent, very kind and attentive service
Excellent presentation

decomposed.text: Wonderful restaurant. I've had Ethiopian food many, many times and the food at this restaurant is top of the list for among the best I've ever eaten. The restaurant has a beautiful ambience, very nice, warm decor. Excellent presentation, and incredibly delicious. It is quite spicy, as the name implies, but not overbearing. Excellent, very kind and attentive service. It's been a long time since I've even felt compelled to write a restaurant review, but this place is quite special. A lovely place that's perfect for a date. I'll be returning for sure.

decomposed.author: Simarjot Sidhu

dimensions.claim: The ambiance is beautiful and warm
The staff were wonderful! So welcoming, helpful, and genuinely kind
The menu has an amazing selection, and everything we tried was outstanding
The menu has an amazing selection

decomposed.text:
A fantastic new Ethiopian restaurant, full of charm and soul! The staff were wonderful! So welcoming, helpful, and genuinely kind.

The menu has an amazing selection, and everything we tried was outstanding. The vegan platter was excellent, and the meat platter and doro tibs were incredible, we keep wanting to come back for more!
The injera and lentils were absolutely amazing, pairs perfectly together. And we were welcomed with crispy injera bread and house-made hot sauces to start, as well as extra injira during our meal. We also tried some traditional drinks (can’t remember the names!). One made with sunflower seeds and another with flaxseed, both unique and delicious.

The ambiance is beautiful and warm. I truly hope to see this restaurant thrive. Highly recommend!

decomposed.author: Israa

dimensions.claim: Food was below to on par with average taste
portion size
Good ambience
Attentive service

decomposed.text:
Food was below to on par with average taste and portion size. Meat done better than vegetarian dishes. Good ambience. Attentive service. Location more accessible than alternatives. If you have time, consider wandering up the road to Ethiopian House.

——-
Regarding the frankly disrespectful and unprofessional response below by an erratic owner lacking fundamental language comprehension: portion sizes are objective and irrespective of group sizes. I have been to several Ethiopian restaurants across different cities, and reflect only objectively on my experiences. If you must know, in the middle of the downpour in Toronto, I made the mistake of encouraging my family to try Ethiopian food for the first time at this establishment since it was a 10 minute walk compared to the 20 to Ethiopian House from Eaton, and we got samplers with the intention of continuing our meal with which dishes resonated the most with them, but found that the taste was unimpressive and thus continued our meal elsewhere. Also, below to average taste does not mean inedible, especially not with a downpour outside. I even told your server the food was “ok” and she was content with this answer. Stars reduced because of disappointing response. How bizarre!

decomposed.author: Kevin Johar

dimensions.claim: Such a warm welcoming environment
The owner, Gelila is incredibly hospitable
it was incredible
We got the Vegan and Meat Sampler plates, which is a great way to try all the different dishes

decomposed.text: Went with my mom for Mother's Day yesterday. This is a local staple for me now. The owner, Gelila is incredibly hospitable. Such a warm welcoming environment. When it comes to food, it was incredible. My mom had it for the first time and LOVED it. We got the Vegan and Meat Sampler plates, which is a great way to try all the different dishes. Hidden gem in the core of downtown!

results.intent: Classic New York slice, thin crust, good fold, no fancy toppings, quick and cheap

relevantReviews.author: SL Wong
relevantReviews.excerpt: The pizzas were absolutely delicious.  I would highly recommend getting the classic cheese. It was probably one of the best pizzas I've tasted.  The crust was thin and doesnt gets soft.
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: This review is relevant because it specifically mentions the quality of the pizza and the crust, which aligns with the user's intent of looking for a classic New York slice with thin crust.

relevantReviews.author: Highway Video
relevantReviews.excerpt: Simple, classic and full of that authentic New York pizza vibe. The slices were fresh, cheesy and so good — exactly the kind of pizza you imagine having in NYC.
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: This review is relevant because it mentions the authenticity and classic nature of the pizza, which aligns with the user's intent.

relevantReviews.author: Girish Wangikar
relevantReviews.excerpt: The prices are reasonable, service is super fast.
relevantReviews.whyRelevant: This review is relevant because it mentions the price value and wait time, which are also important to the user.

analysis.explanation: For someone looking for a classic New York slice with thin crust, good fold, and no fancy toppings, this restaurant scores slightly higher than Google's 4.4 because 3 out of 5 reviewers praised the food quality, with sentiments ranging from 0.8 to 0.9, and 2 reviewers mentioned the authenticity of the pizza, with sentiments of 0.6 and 0.7. Additionally, reviewers mentioned reasonable prices and quick service, which align with the user's intent. Google's high rating is driven by service and ambiance scores, which the user deprioritized, but the dimensions that matter most to this user are well-represented in the reviews, resulting in a slightly higher Parallax score.

decomposed.author: Girish Wangikar

dimensions.claim: everything was amazing
The prices are reasonable
service is super fast
Locations are spread across the city
You might not get place to sit inside

decomposed.text: As a tourists visited the highly recommended Joe’s pizza and it didn’t let me down. Had multiple slices across three days and everything was amazing. The prices are reasonable, service is super fast. Locations are spread across the city. You might not get place to sit inside so better to carry to nearby parks where you can eat it in peace. I went early in the morning when the crowd was manageable but later on people started pouring in and it wad nonstop.

decomposed.author: SL Wong

dimensions.claim: The pizzas were absolutely delicious
the queues get really longer after that
They will heat up your slices after you've ordered

decomposed.text:
This is the main outlet of Joe's pizza.  If you don't want to queue, come before dinner as the queues get really longer after that. Even at 9 pm the queue gets even longer.

The left shop sells pizza by the slice and the right shop(also Joe's pizza) sells them by pie (whole pizza).

The pizzas were absolutely delicious.  I would highly recommend getting the classic cheese. It was probably one of the best pizzas I've tasted.  The crust was thin and doesnt gets soft.

They will heat up your slices after you've ordered.

decomposed.author: Highway Video

dimensions.claim: The slices were fresh, cheesy and so good
Simple, classic and full of that authentic New York pizza vibe
Be prepared for a queue though
Quick service once inside

decomposed.text:
A must-visit if you’re in New York.
Simple, classic and full of that authentic New York pizza vibe. The slices were fresh, cheesy and so good — exactly the kind of pizza you imagine having in NYC.
Be prepared for a queue though. There’s almost always a line and the wait can easily be around 30–45 minutes, but the pizza is definitely worth it.
Quick service once inside and perfect for grabbing a slice while exploring the city. Definitely lived up to the hype.

decomposed.author: Bruno g

dimensions.claim: The pizza was good, but not outstanding for me
the service felt very fast-paced and almost “assembly line” style
it doesn’t really allow you to take your time to enjoy the experience if you’re eating on site
adds to the “iconic New York spot” feeling

decomposed.text:
My experience at Joe’s Pizza on Broadway was a bit mixed.

First of all, the service felt very fast-paced and almost “assembly line” style. Everything is designed to move quickly, which makes sense given how popular the place is, but it doesn’t really allow you to take your time to enjoy the experience if you’re eating on site. You order, receive your slice, and things move on immediately.

In terms of taste, I honestly expected something more exceptional given the reputation. The pizza was good, but not outstanding for me. It didn’t quite match the level of hype I had built up before coming here.

I also get the impression that a big part of its popularity comes from its appearance in Spider-Man and the celebrity photos on the walls, which definitely adds to the “iconic New York spot” feeling.

To be clear, it wasn’t bad at all — just not as memorable as I expected considering all the reputation around it. I was simply expecting a bit more in terms of flavor and overall experience.

Still, it’s an iconic place worth trying once if you’re in New York, especially for the history and pop culture aspect.

decomposed.author: Salman Idrees

dimensions.claim: The crust was perfectly crisp on the outside while staying soft and chewy inside
the atmosphere was warm, lively, and welcoming
The staff greeted me with genuine smiles and were incredibly friendly and attentive throughout my visit

decomposed.text:
I just had to share my amazing experience at Joe’s Pizza — it truly exceeded all my expectations!

From the moment I walked in, the atmosphere was warm, lively, and welcoming. The staff greeted me with genuine smiles and were incredibly friendly and attentive throughout my visit. It’s clear they take pride not just in their food but in making customers feel valued and comfortable.

Now let’s talk about the pizza — absolutely phenomenal. The crust was perfectly crisp on the outside while staying soft and chewy inside. The sauce had that rich, balanced flavor that tastes fresh and homemade, and the cheese was melted to perfection with just the right stretch in every bite. Every slice was packed with flavor, and you can really tell they use high-quality ingredients.

Service was fast and efficient even though the place was busy, which says a lot about how well the team works together. The staff were patient, helpful with recommendations, and made the whole experience even better.

If you’re looking for authentic, delicious pizza served by a team that genuinely cares, Joe’s Pizza is a must-visit. I’ll definitely be coming back for more!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Highly recommend

decomposed.author: Micheal

dimensions.claim: everything arrived warm and well-prepared
Our server Renee was excellent, attentive, professional, and on top of everything throughout the meal
The greeters were equally welcoming and accommodated our booth request without any hassle
the noise level, Some guests were incredibly loud
Washrooms were clean as well
The complimentary ice cream cake was a great surprise and much appreciated

decomposed.text:
Came here for a birthday dinner and had a great experience overall, much better than my visit to the King Street location a few days prior, where the food came out cold. Here, everything arrived warm and well-prepared.

Our server Renee was excellent, attentive, professional, and on top of everything throughout the meal. The greeters were equally welcoming and accommodated our booth request without any hassle. When I asked the greeter who seated us to take some photos, she was happy to help, which was a nice touch for a birthday occasion.

The complimentary ice cream cake was a great surprise and much appreciated. Washrooms were clean as well, which always matters.

The only thing worth mentioning is the noise level. Some guests were incredibly loud, the kind where you feel like you’re sitting next to a theme park ride rather than a dinner table. That said, it has nothing to do with the staff or the restaurant itself, so it doesn’t take away from the 5 stars. Everything within their control was handled really well.

Would definitely return.

decomposed.author: Ann

dimensions.claim: Our food arrived very quickly within about 10 minutes
Everything was incredibly fresh and very tasty
I also had a cocktail that was perfectly made and complemented the meal nicely
The atmosphere of the restaurant is beautiful and very welcoming

decomposed.text:
I visited Keg Restaurant for lunch with a friend and had a wonderful experience. Our food arrived very quickly  within about 10 minutes  which was impressive.
We ordered a few appetizers, including the tartare, and also tried the grilled seafood. Everything was incredibly fresh and very tasty. I also had a cocktail that was perfectly made and complemented the meal nicely.
The atmosphere of the restaurant is beautiful and very welcoming. Overall, it was a lovely lunch, and I truly enjoyed my time there. I would definitely come back again.

decomposed.author: TheTkhan

dimensions.claim: we had the same waiter as last time probably one of the best waiters ever gave us top notch service
I ordered the calamari appetizer delicious as always, crispy and tender on the inside
The prime rib was cooked to perfection, and massive
the waiter who served us before recognized us and even gave us a free billy miner pie as it was me and wife's anniversary

decomposed.text:
I managed to get a a reservation for Valentine's Day, and we had the same waiter as last time probably one of the best waiters ever gave us top notch service and my daughter was smiling at him as well.

I ordered the calamari appetizer delicious as always, crispy and tender on the inside.

I ordered the 18 oz prime rib with mash potatoes , with the red wine jus and fried onions and horseradish.

The prime rib was cooked to perfection, and massive and the waiter who served us before recognized us and even gave us a free billy miner pie as it was me and wife's anniversary.

The horseradish is strong eat with caution but the prime rib was fantastic really heavy I don't think I have to eat for a week honestly. Keg always been a staple and consistent

decomposed.author: Doni

dimensions.claim: I really enjoyed the service. Our server was very polite
The nachos with beef on top were amazing — the toppings matched perfectly, and the portion size was great for 2–3 people
Their pitcher of sangria also tasted really good and came in a generous portion
I was disappointed with the steak. I ordered the prime rib, and it had a lot of fat on the sides

decomposed.text:
It was actually my first time visiting The Keg Steakhouse. I usually go to all-you-can-eat steakhouses, so I always wondered why people would pay for just a single steak. I really enjoyed the service. Our server was very polite, and I also liked how the chef personally brought out the dishes. The nachos with beef on top were amazing — the toppings matched perfectly, and the portion size was great for 2–3 people. Their pitcher of sangria also tasted really good and came in a generous portion.
However, I was disappointed with the steak. I ordered the prime rib, and it had a lot of fat on the sides. Maybe because of that, it didn’t taste as good as people usually say about this restaurant. I’ve honestly never seen a steak with that much fat before. I asked for medium rare, it leaned more toward rare, it was juicy enough tho.  Maybe their other steak options are better, but if I had known, I probably wouldn’t have ordered the prime rib.

decomposed.author: Dina Bakhit

dimensions.claim: Their customer service is truly top-notch. They went above and beyond to make sure every detail of our evening was perfect
Marline and Will, constantly checking in and making sure we were enjoying our dinner

decomposed.text: The Keg Steakhouse Leslie location.  We keep coming back, and a big part of that is thanks to Marline and Will. Their customer service is truly top-notch. They went above and beyond to make sure every detail of our evening was perfect, constantly checking in and making sure we were enjoying our dinner. Their attentiveness and genuine care made the whole experience feel extra special. Service like that is hard to find, and it’s what makes this place stand out every time we visit. Keep up the amazing work and thank you from my family for always welcoming us with a smile.

Patient staff and kid-friendly.

dimensions.claim: Excellent meal
Excellent service

Standouts were definitley the daal, chana and karahi.
The chai however could be stronger, it is very milky and doesnt have much tea flavour.

decomposed.text: Excellent meal and service despite how busy it was. Everything was really good. We ordered the chicken karahi, chana masala, seekh kabob, chicken tikka, daal and fried fish. Standouts were definitley the daal, chana and karahi. Unfortunately they were out of the ribs and goat chops but I will make sure to get it next time. The kulfi was also really good! The chai however could be stronger, it is very milky and doesnt have much tea flavour.

decomposed.author: Shay Gray

dimensions.claim: the taste remained the same
The portion size was also pretty good
the loveliest aroma

decomposed.text: I have been here a few times and I can’t say I have any complaints. I actually ordered through Uber Eats this time and can say the taste remained the same! In fact, as soon as I opened the container it had the loveliest aroma. The portion size was also pretty good, though the salad was layered on the rice lol which was actually fine. Also, a solid choice eating and ordering from here.

decomposed.author: Ahmed Sachwani

dimensions.claim: The food was absolutely delicious.

decomposed.text: It was our first visit and we're so glad we came. The food was absolutely delicious. Just the right balance of spice. The staff was friendly and very attentive. I would definitely come back for the nihari and kababs.

decomposed.author: Family Room

dimensions.claim: the food is swimming in flavour.
the restaurant is clean
the service is prompt and friendly

decomposed.text: Possibly the best Pakistani restaurant in Ontario! Bold statement, but true. First and foremost - the food is swimming in flavour. Doesn’t matter what you get, I guarantee it will be delicious 😋 Secondly, the restaurant is clean and the service is prompt and friendly. We don’t live in the area but whenever we’re passing by, we make time for lunch or dinner here. We’re never disappointed and neither will you.

decomposed.text:
Our coffees were awful! Way too hot, burnt and un-drinkable. We threw them out.

The bacon, egg and cheese biscuit was undercooked and doughy and the egg tasted old and chewy - the bacon and cheese were tasty though.

The sausage and egg bagel was delicious!

decomposed.author: Dharven Panchal

dimensions.claim: That is average McDonald's store and outlet for you
It is located on the prime location nearby to Times Square
Only one burger is there for vegeterian people that only you have to do customisation
No parking area available

decomposed.text: That is average McDonald's store and outlet for you. You can only go for if you want some quick bite. It is located on the prime location nearby to Times Square. They have both done in and take away options available. You can order on the kiosk and wait for your order. The first floor is for dine in area. Only one burger is there for vegeterian people that only you have to do customisation. No parking area available. Rush hours will have much traffic here.

decomposed.author: Elvis Jimenez

dimensions.claim: One of the cleanest mc'D in NYC
very fast service
hot food and hot coffee

decomposed.text: One of the cleanest mc'D in NYC very fast service hot food and hot coffee. Worth the trip

decomposed.author: VinVin C

dimensions.claim: Every time I order an iced coffee, it never consistent
I really don't understand why this times square location is so bad at this and almost never adds sugar
If it's for kids or young people, they usually prefer sweeter drinks

decomposed.text: I have a complaint. Every time I order an iced coffee, it never consistent. Sometimes there's syrup added, but most of the time there's no sugar at all. If it's for kids or young people, they usually prefer sweeter drinks, and iced coffee is supposed to be a sweet beverage. So I really don't understand why this times square location is so bad at this and almost never adds sugar. It feels like I am wasting my money on a coffee that isn't made properly. If I didn't want sugar, I would have ordered a hot coffee instead.

decomposed.author: GHD_1109

dimensions.claim: McDonald’s Times Square is a good stop if you’re near Times Square
The upstairs seating is nice

decomposed.text: McDonald’s Times Square is a good stop if you’re near Times Square. The upstairs seating is nice, but you have to buy something to go up there.

dimensions.claim: The food quality was excellent
The restaurant has a comfortable atmosphere
The service was excellent
They have many Japanese dishes that are very hard to find in Toronto
They have many premium Japanese sake that you don’t usually see outside Japan

decomposed.text:
Had a great dinner at Ju-Raku recently. The food quality was excellent and they have many Japanese dishes that are very hard to find in Toronto.

The Kinmedai sashimi was super fresh and sweet. The uni was rich and creamy, and the otoro was amazing. The oysters were also very fresh. One of my favourite dishes was the grilled fish fin, which is not something you can easily find in other Japanese restaurants. The mentaiko chicken skewers were also very tasty.

I was also impressed by their sake selection. They have many premium Japanese sake that you don’t usually see outside Japan, and it was nice to try something different from the usual sake available in most restaurants.

The service was excellent as well. The staff were very friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable about both the food and sake. They took the time to explain the dishes and gave good recommendations, which made the whole dining experience even better.

The restaurant has a comfortable atmosphere and is a good place for both special occasions and casual dinners.

If you enjoy authentic Japanese food, fresh seafood, rare Japanese dishes, and good sake, I highly recommend Ju-Raku. Definitely one of my favourite Japanese restaurants in Toronto. Will definitely come back again! 🍣🍶👍🏻✨

decomposed.author: Hank Ban

dimensions.claim: Tuna nigiri was surprisingly good
Inside the mall. Ample seating and well lit, clean and nice modern decor
Service was pretty good
Doesn't justify the price they're charging
Clean and nice modern decor

decomposed.text:
Tuna nigiri was surprisingly good. Every large platter of sushi, they throw in these pre-prepared California rolls that are cold and dry to make plate seem fuller. It's nice if you're gong for quantity but it brings the quality level down. Appetizers were meh. Hamachi carpace tasted a little odd.

Service was pretty good. Inside the mall. Ample seating and well lit, clean and nice modern decor. Separate room for teppanyaki which is nice as those tend to get noisy. Thing off is they have random storage out in the open - also another point off for the place's overall quality. Use of plastic serving trays for some of their fish also brings down the quality feel despite colorful patterns including potentially affecting the taste. They try hard for the upscale look for other things like having monogrammed place settings and crane chopsticks rest. Therefore all-in-all, it doesn't justify the price they're charging. Maybe for teppanyaki but I don't believe so for their sushi. I think they could be better and do have potential. May explain partly why they weren't overly busy when I went.

decomposed.author: Paul Murata

dimensions.claim: The teppanyaki was average, and the chawanmushi missed the mark entirely
The vibe from the start suggested it's a Chinese-run establishment serving Japanese-style food
It’s more Japanese-adjacent than authentically Japanese
The Winterlicious lunch teppanyaki menu was reasonably priced
I’d recommend it for families with young children

decomposed.text:
My excitement for Ju-Raku was high on Valentine's Day. As a newly opened spot with a sleek online presence, it seemed like a great choice. However, from the moment we arrived, it was clear that the reality didn't match the high-end image we had built up in our minds.

Follow post owner review. There will not be a return visit.
To be clear, a more casual atmosphere isn't a bad thing. But when a restaurant requires your credit card information to secure a reservation, it sets a certain expectation for the level of service and quality. Unfortunately, the online portrayal felt misleading compared to our in-person experience.

The restaurant is presented as Japanese, but the vibe from the start suggested it's a Chinese-run establishment serving Japanese-style food. That’s not inherently negative, but it became a defining characteristic of the meal. The sushi rice was a pleasant surprise—well-prepared—and the fish was fresh, but that was the peak of the culinary experience. The teppanyaki was average, and the chawanmushi missed the mark entirely. In my opinion, it lacked the delicate subtlety that defines Japanese cuisine; it felt like a poor translation of the dish, with odd ingredient additions and, most notably, the absence of the traditional ginkgo nut.

The miso soup arrived lukewarm, which was disappointing. The green salad, while refreshing, was also quite small. On the positive side, the Winterlicious lunch teppanyaki menu was reasonably priced, and the quality of the meats was good, though the Wagyu fat was chewy and its texture inconsistent.

It’s possible the restaurant is still finding its footing, being so new. But the impression it left was less of a place perfecting its craft and more of one experimenting to see what sells, without a strong focus on delivering a refined product.

This brings me back to the credit card requirement for a reservation. I fail to see its necessity unless a restaurant is either a highly exclusive, sought-after venue or overly concerned about preventing no-shows. Ju-Raku is neither.

In summary, Ju-Raku isn’t a bad restaurant. It’s more Japanese-adjacent than authentically Japanese. I’d recommend it for families with young children who might enjoy the teppanyaki show and the reasonable prices. For anyone seeking a genuine Japanese dining experience, I’d suggest looking elsewhere.

decomposed.author: Michele Marques

dimensions.claim: The food is a little pricey but absolutely delicious
The atmosphere is elegant, with beautiful murals and unusual lighting
The level of service is very mixed
The food is a little pricey
The timing of delivering dishes was odd

decomposed.text:
I finally tried Ju-Raku for lunch, and I will definitely be back. The atmosphere is elegant, with beautiful murals and unusual lighting.

The food is a little pricey but absolutely delicious. The hamachi in the daily special handroll was so tender and fresh.

My only complaint is that the level of service is very mixed. On the positive side, the waiter asked about allergies, and told us about one item having dairy that we did not expect. However, the timing of delivering dishes was odd.  I realize that food might not be ready at the same time, but why did one of us get our miso soup and salad about 15 minutes before the other?

decomposed.author: Joanne Cheung (Hungry.Jo)

dimensions.claim: The sushi is the freshest and the assortment flown in from Japan and Europe
Comfortable and spacious seating for our party of 6
The service was excellent
We ordered the Sashimi Moriawase ( 30 pieces for $99)
Generous portion served in a traditional iron Japanese pot

decomposed.text:
We had our Christmas gathering here and the service was excellent and everyone was very impressed with the food. Comfortable and spacious seating for our party of 6.

We ordered the Sashimi Moriawase ( 30 pieces for $99). The sushi is the freshest and the assortment flown in from Japan and Europe. The Otoro was amazing and melts in your mouth.

We ordered some maki rolls and were a good size and right proportions of rice. We highly recommend the Lobster Maki along with the Golden Dragon Maki.

We also enjoyed the Steamed Clams in sake and Kombu broth. Generous portion served in a traditional iron Japanese pot.

For curry Udon, we selected the beef for our protein and we were pleasantly surprised topped with a perfectly grilled steak.

For apps we included GYOZAS and shrimp and vegetable Tempura.

If you haven’t been to Ju Raku located inside Bayview Village in North York, it’s time to make a visit in your the New Year

analysis.explanation: For someone looking for authentic Mexican street tacos with real al pastor and corn tortillas, this restaurant scores slightly lower than Google's 4.4 because 2 of 5 reviewers praised the flavor and authenticity of the tacos, but 1 reviewer had a negative experience with a burrito. Google's high rating is driven by service and ambiance scores, which this user deprioritized. The Parallax score is 4.1, 0.3 lower than Google's, due to the focus on food quality and authenticity.
